Page 7, Section 2.1.1 (Regularized bicausal OT), Remark 2.1

"Remark 2.1. This paper focuses on existence and uniqueness of solutions introduced in Definition 2.2. Convergence of numerical algorithms for the transport problem with time-inconsistent costs remains an problem. Notably, even in the static case with quadratic regularization, this problem has only been examined in a recent work by Gonz\´alez-Sanz and Nutz (2024)."
title: Convergence of numerical algorithms for time-inconsistent (bi)causal transport
latex:
Consider a finite time horizon \(T\in\mathbb{N}\), Polish state spaces \(\mathcal{X}_t,\mathcal{Y}_t\) for \(t=1,\dots,T\), and path spaces \(\mathcal{X}:=\prod_{t=1}^T \mathcal{X}_t\), \(\mathcal{Y}:=\prod_{t=1}^T \mathcal{Y}_t\). Let \(\mu\in\mathcal{P}(\mathcal{X})\) and \(\nu\in\mathcal{P}(\mathcal{Y})\) be given marginals, and let \(\Pi_{\mathrm{bc}}(\mu,\nu)\) denote the set of bicausal couplings between \(\mu\) and \(\nu\).

Fix a (possibly time-inconsistent) cost functional \(J_0:\Pi_{\mathrm{bc}}(\mu,\nu)\to\mathbb{R}\cup\{+\infty\}\) (e.g., the non-separable \(f\)-divergence regularized objective in equation (2.1)–(2.3), or a nonlinear/state-dependent objective as in (2.4)–(2.6)). Let \(\pi^\star\in\Pi_{\mathrm{bc}}(\mu,\nu)\) be an equilibrium transport plan in the sense of Definition 2.2 (a subgame-perfect Nash equilibrium in successive one-step deviations).

Design and analyze numerical algorithms \(\{\pi^{(n)}\}_{n\ge 1}\subset\Pi_{\mathrm{bc}}(\mu,\nu)\) that are intended to compute \(\pi^\star\) (or the associated equilibrium kernels), and establish verifiable conditions under which the iterates converge to an equilibrium transport, e.g.
\[
\pi^{(n)} \to \pi^\star \quad \text{in an appropriate topology/metric on }\Pi_{\mathrm{bc}}(\mu,\nu),
\]
possibly together with convergence of values \(J_0(\pi^{(n)})\to J_0(\pi^\star)\) and/or convergence of the induced equilibrium value functions.

The problem is to provide such convergence guarantees (rates if possible) for numerical methods for equilibrium transport under time-inconsistent costs (including non-separable \(f\)-divergence regularization), extending what is currently known even for static quadratically regularized optimal transport.
open_problem_quote: "Remark 2.1. This paper focuses on existence and uniqueness of solutions introduced in Definition 2.2. Convergence of numerical algorithms for the transport problem with time-inconsistent costs remains an problem. Notably, even in the static case with quadratic regularization, this problem has only been examined in a recent work by Gonz\´alez-Sanz and Nutz (2024)."

verification.notice: This entry was generated from the paper text only. It has not been independently verified as still open. If you believe the question is solved or incorrectly stated, please contact the maintainers.
